source_refs.rationale: HMRC getting-started guidance still directs developers to create a sandbox application, choose APIs, get credentials, and test in sandbox.
HMRC sandbox guidance says applications must be subscribed to APIs before they can access them and confirms sandbox credentials are managed separately afterwards.
HMRC Income Tax MTD minimum-functionality guidance distinguishes the in-year required API set from later end-of-year APIs.
HMRC naming guidance allows free-form sandbox names now while still requiring production-safe naming discipline later.
HMRC development practices say one production application is usually sufficient and traffic isolation should come from OAuth tokens rather than multiple application registrations.

required_now.requirement_rationale: Required now because the current Taxat authority profile catalog maps reference and income-source discovery to Business Details, and HMRC's in-year product guidance includes it in the minimum required API set.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance includes Business Details in the in-year required API set.

required_now.requirement_rationale: Required now because the roadmap slice explicitly covers obligations and the authority profile catalog binds obligation reads to this API family.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance includes Obligations in the in-year required API set.

portal_label_aliases: Income Tax calculations and final declaration
required_now.requirement_rationale: Required now because this roadmap slice explicitly includes calculations and submissions, and the current authority catalog binds calculation retrieval and final-declaration-capable calculation flows to Individual Calculations.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance includes Individual Calculations for software that surfaces calculations in-product rather than signposting customers away.

required_now.requirement_rationale: Required now because the current roadmap slice covers submission surfaces, and the Taxat authority catalog binds periodic updates and post-finalisation amendments for self-employment businesses to this API.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance requires Self-Employment Business and/or Property Business for the in-year product.

required_now.requirement_rationale: Required now because the current roadmap slice covers submission surfaces, and the Taxat authority catalog binds periodic updates and post-finalisation amendments for property businesses to this API.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance requires Self-Employment Business and/or Property Business for the in-year product.

likely_required_later.requirement_rationale: Deferred to later scope because HMRC places BSAS in the end-of-year product, not the narrow in-year obligations-calculations-submissions slice implemented by this card.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance lists BSAS in the end-of-year required API set.

likely_required_later.requirement_rationale: Deferred to later scope because HMRC places Individual Losses in the end-of-year product, not the narrow in-year obligations-calculations-submissions slice implemented by this card.

source_refs.rationale: Current live API version, stage, and environment endpoints.
HMRC minimum-functionality guidance lists Individual Losses in the end-of-year required API set.

typed_decisions.rationale: This roadmap slice explicitly includes calculations and submissions, so the safer interpretation is to keep in-software calculation capability in scope rather than defer it.
HMRC places BSAS in the end-of-year product and the current card stops at obligations, calculations, and submission-enabling sandbox subscriptions.
HMRC places loss-claim support in the end-of-year product and the current card intentionally avoids broadening this slice beyond the canonical in-year set.

notes: The current Taxat sandbox baseline intentionally subscribes both Self Employment Business and Property Business so the canonical sandbox app can support the broader authority-profile matrix without re-registration later.
Client credentials are intentionally out of scope for this card even though HMRC sandbox guidance exposes them after application creation and subscription.
